content.question:
Put the verbs in brackets into an appropriate tense.

Jim (1) WAS NOT LOOKING forward to the exam. He (2) ___ (study) for the past two months, and still (3) ___ (not/feel) sure that he (4) ___ (know) even half of what he should know. He (5) ___ (question) his teachers repeatedly about the material that (6) ___ (appear) on past exams, but he still (7) ___ (not/be) convinced that anything he (8) ___ (learn) (9) ___ (be) relevant to this year's exam. He (10) ___ (still/study) at seven o'clock in the morning on the day of the exam; he (11) ___ (revise) all night long, and (12) ___ (feel) exhausted. In fact he (13) ___ (be) so tired that he (14) ___ (fall) asleep in the middle of writing the exam, and (15) ___ (wake up) only just in time to finish it.
content.answer: had been studying ; did not feel ; knew ; had questioned / had been questioning ; had appeared ; was not ; had learnt ; would be / was ; was still studying ; had been revising ; felt ; was ; fell ; woke up

content.question:
Put the verbs in brackets into an appropriate tense.

In the last few years there (16) HAS BEEN (be) a marked change in our attitude towards environmental issues. It (17) ___ (start) with scientists revealing that a hole (18) ___ (begin) to develop in the ozone layer above the Arctic Circle; when people (19) ___ (start) to notice changes in the climate, it (20) ___ (become) obvious that there (21) ___ (be) a serious threat. Those of us who (22) ___ (ever/use) an aerosol spray will presumably think twice before using one again. And what (23) ___ (happen) to all that nuclear waste we have produced? Presumably most of it (24) ___ (be/dump) into the sea, but the number of protesting voices (25) ___ (increase) steadily year by year.
content.answer: started ; has begun / had begun ; started ; became ; was ; have ever used ; has been happening ; has been dumped ; has been increasing

content.question:
Put the verbs in brackets into an appropriate past tense.

If it (26) HAD NOT BEEN for Louis, Joan (27) ___ (never/survive) her trip to Paris last month. She (28) ___ (not/meet) him before, but she (29) ___ (be) certainly glad that she had by the end of her stay. The first thing that (30) ___ (happen) was that the hotel where she (31) ___ (plan) to stay (32) ___ (never/receive) her booking, so they had no room for her. Then, as she (33) ___ (try) to get a taxi to take her to another hotel, someone on a motorbike (34) ___ (snatch) her bag with all her tickets and credit cards in it. As her French (35) ___ (be) quite rusty, she (36) ___ (not/know) how to explain what (37) ___ (happen). It was then that Louis (38) ___ (approach) her and (39) ___ (introduce) himself.
content.answer: would never have survived ; had not met ; was ; happened ; had been planning / had planned ; had never received ; was trying ; snatched ; was ; did not know ; had happened ; approached ; introduced
